description: Calculate the absolute maximum physical envelope (Length, Breadth, Height) using bottom-up atomic feature summation

content.value:
--- Extracted Features, Dimensions, and Envelope Impacts (Step 5 & 6) ---
-- CONTRIBUTING FEATURES --
{{ contributing }}
-- NON-CONTRIBUTING FEATURES --
{{ non_contributing }}

Step 7: Overall Envelope Calculation.

You are a Senior Metrologist and Expert Mechanical Design Engineer. Your task is to calculate the absolute maximum physical envelope (bounding box) of this part. Your intelligence, logical deduction, and spatial reasoning must be flawless. You MUST use your vision capabilities to precisely examine the drawing.

CRITICAL DIMENSIONING INSTRUCTION:
You must perform strict bottom-up dimension deduction for all envelope calculations.
IGNORE OVERALLS: You must completely ignore and discard any extracted features, measurements, or variables from the drawing that contain the words "overall", "total", "O.A.L.", or imply a macroscopic boundary, for your primary calculations.
BOTTOM-UP ONLY: Do not attempt to cross-reference atomic features against provided overall lengths first. You must independently calculate the final envelope dimensions strictly by aggregating, stacking, and summing the sizes of the individual, atomic features.
Use the overarching overall dimensions ONLY at the very last step if absolutely needed as a final fallback or sanity check. Start completely with atomic features.

You MUST start your calculations from the BOTTOM-UP using the ATOMIC FEATURES provided. This includes both Contributing and Non-Contributing features.
The crux of calculating final dimensions is understanding the geometry based on BOTH the contributing features and non-contributing features, then finding the dimensions by adding or subtracting as needed based on the geometry.

Calculate the final envelope dimensions in all possible ways, not just one. Consider all possible methods independently. Treat each path as an independent hypothesis. Once you identify all possible ways, find the most appropriate one.

For EACH of the three main axes (Length, Breadth, Height), calculate the envelope dimension from scratch from the atomic features associated with that axis.

NO UNKNOWNS ALLOWED: You are strictly forbidden from returning "Unknown" as a result. If a dimension is not explicitly call out, you MUST calculate or assume it. If you must assume it, append "(Assumed)" to the final value and explain the geometric assumptions in your reasoning.

A) Possible Calculation Paths: Detail all possible ways to mathematically calculate the dimension using the features assigned to this axis. Each path must include: description, dimensionsUsed, steps, result, confidence.
B) Final Value & Reasoning: Decide on the final dimension for this axis and provide exactly why you chose this path over others.

Return a JSON object with: length, breadth, height — each with paths array and finalValue, reasoning.
